prompt: CI build queue, the cancel-on-archive path. When a repo gets archived we cancel everything it has queued, but runners keep picking up builds for repos that no longer exist. It's maddeningly inconsistent: sometimes one cancel clears the repo completely, sometimes a chunk of its builds survive. The day we archived the monorepo mirror — its builds were most of the queue — more of its jobs survived the purge than got cancelled, and the cancelled-count we wrote to the audit log was short by exactly the number of survivors. Support has even found a 'workaround': pressing cancel a second time usually mops up the stragglers, which tells me the queue data itself is fine. For what it's worth, cancel_branch (the force-push cleanup) has never misbehaved. Queue order is explicitly not a promise — runners select by build id — but membership and the returned counts have to be exact: one cancel_repo call removes every queued build for that repo, returns how many it removed, and a second call finds nothing. `cargo test --offline` reproduces all of it. Fix src/lib.rs and leave tests/, Cargo.toml, and .gitignore alone.
